content.full_text:
ROY F. KROLL & ASSOCIATES, INC.

Residential Architecture

Custom Architectural Design • Renovation & Addition Design
Historic Preservation • Project Management

Roy F. Kroll, Architect


April 8, 1992




Fair Political Practices Commission
PO Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95812

Attn: Ms. Louisa Menchaca, Legislation Counsel

Dear Ms. Menchaca,

Would you please confirm our telephone conservation of today regarding contributions to appointed City commissioners for their City Council Candidacy. As a Architect who must submit his projects to the City for approval and the process usually involves an appointed commission and being that there are Commissioners who are running for City Council, I wish to avoid a Conflict of Interest. You advised that there would be NO conflict of interest as long as contributions were less than $250.00, and the candidate would not have to step down during proceedings on an architectural project which I may bring forward for the "Commission" for approval as long as I have not contributed to his income in some other business way.

Thank you for your kind assistance.

Sincerely,

ROY F. KROLL & ASSOCIATES, INC.

Roy F. Kroll, A.I.A. A.I.C.P
President

RFK/rfk


1291 North Indian Hill Boulevard • Claremont, CA 91711 • Fax 714/625-7807 • Tel 714/625-5888

sections.question: Would you please confirm our telephone conversation of today regarding contributions to appointed City commissioners for their City Council Candidacy. As a Architect who must submit his projects to the City for approval and the process usually involves an appointed commission and being that there are Commissioners who are running for City Council, I wish to avoid a Conflict of Interest. You advised that there would be NO conflict of interest as long as contributions were less than $250.00, and the candidate would not have to step down during proceedings on an architectural project which I may bring forward for the "Commission" for approval as long as I have not contributed to his income in some other business way.
sections.question_synthetic: Does an architect creating contributions under $250 to city commissioners running for city council while submitting projects to those same commissioners for approval create a conflict of interest, or can the commissioners participate in decisions on the architect's projects if no other financial relationship exists?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request/correspondence letter from a private citizen seeking confirmation of prior FPPC telephone advice. No FPPC response or formal sections are present. The document references prior telephone counsel but does not contain an official FPPC advice letter response. Confidence moderate due to informal format and reference to oral advice rather than written FPPC guidance.

embedding.summary: Architect Roy F. Kroll requests confirmation of a telephone conversation with FPPC counsel regarding potential conflicts of interest arising from contributions to city commissioners who may rule on his architectural projects submitted for city approval.
